hypothesis: If a conditional latent diffusion model trained on the CIAE latent space and conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed element lists can generate shell-wise catalytic surface compositions that (i) closely reproduce the real data distribution (lower Fréchet Distance), (ii) satisfy chemical constraints (low Leakage), and (iii) achieve high point-wise quality (high Cosine Similarity) compared to a chemically-agnostic baseline, then the CompGen framework enables effective inverse composition design of catalytic surfaces and its performance scales with training data.
